DISCOVERY 612, APPEARANCES: ROBERT A. PADWAY, ESQ. FOR PLAINTIFF/MOVING PARTY, TASTE CATERING. HAIG ARSEN HARRIS, JR. FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY. MATTER ARGUED. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR AS THE PROOF OF SERVICE IS INADEQUATE. THE INADEQUACY OF THE PROOF OF SERVICE IS NOT A WAIVABLE DEFECT, AND DOES NOT REQUIRE AN OPPOSITION IN ORDER FOR THE COURT TO MAKE THAT FINDING. THE DECLARATION OF SERVICE BY THE PERSON WHO ACTUALLY MADE SERVICE WAS NOT FILED FIVE DAYS PRIOR TO THE HEARING AS REQUIRED BY CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 317(C). THE PARTIES MUST MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE DATES FOR DEPOSITION. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
NOTICE OF EARLY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE - EARLY SETTLEMENT PROGRAM

DISCOVERY 612, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, IS MOOT. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE LATE RESPONSES WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $301.30, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. LATE OPPOSITION HAS BEEN CONSIDERED. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
